name: Security Baseline
description: Essential security rules for AI assistants working with code and infrastructure. Prevents accidental exposure of secrets, enforces safe defaults, and prompts verification before destructive actions.
author: Instill AI

preferences.rule: Never include secrets, API keys, tokens, or passwords in code, logs, or responses. If you see one, redact it and flag it immediately.
Before executing any destructive operation (DROP, DELETE, rm -rf, reset --hard), state what will be destroyed and wait for explicit confirmation.
Do not commit .env files, credentials, or private keys. Check .gitignore is in place before any git operation.
When generating auth code, use established libraries (bcrypt, argon2, passport) — never roll custom crypto.
Sanitise all user inputs before use in SQL queries, shell commands, or HTML output. Prefer parameterised queries.
Flag any dependency with a known CVE before using or upgrading it. Run npm audit / pip audit as part of setup steps.
